zh:
美洲怪谈没有统一的神谱，它的「神话」散落在殖民时代的农舍、公路边的树线和深夜的论坛帖子里。新泽西松林中的利兹家第十三子、田纳西农场里开口说话的恶灵、哈德逊河谷的无头骑士——这些殖民与拓荒时代的传说，把欧洲移民带来的鬼故事嫁接在新大陆的荒野上。把它们记下来、传出去的是报纸编辑、乡镇警长和围着火堆的邻居。

二十世纪的美洲怪谈围绕「目击」展开：1952年弗拉特伍兹山丘上的黑桃头怪影、1955年围攻肯塔基农舍的银色小生物、1966年欢乐点镇追逐汽车的红眼天蛾人，以及布拉夫溪的巨大脚印。地方报纸的头版、警察局的报案记录和石膏脚模构成了这类传说的「文献」，神秘动物学（cryptozoology）应运而生，大脚怪、欧戈波戈与卓柏卡布拉从乡野口碑变成全国新闻，再变成小镇雕像与年度节庆。

互联网让这片大陆的造神速度提升到以天计：2009年6月10日Something Awful论坛的两张P图诞生了瘦长鬼影，2018年Trevor Henderson的一张合成照片立起了警笛头，2019年5月4chan的一段匿名文字打开了后室的黄色房间。这些角色有确切的创作者与出生日期，却像真正的民间传说一样被千万人接力改写。从血腥玛丽的浴室镜子到「让我们进来」的黑眼孩童，美洲怪谈的气质始终如一：它们发生在你家门口，讲述者总是言之凿凿——「这是我朋友的朋友亲眼看见的」。
en:
American folklore has no unified pantheon; its mythology is scattered across colonial farmhouses, treelines along back roads, and late-night forum posts. The thirteenth child of Mother Leeds in the Jersey pines, the talking spirit on a Tennessee farm, the headless rider of the Hudson Valley — the legends of the colonial and frontier eras grafted the ghost stories of European settlers onto the wilderness of a new continent. Their tellers were newspaper editors, county sheriffs, and neighbors around a fire.

The twentieth century organized American legend around the sighting: the spade-hooded figure on a Flatwoods hilltop in 1952, the silvery beings besieging a Kentucky farmhouse in 1955, the red-eyed Mothman chasing cars outside Point Pleasant in 1966, the great footprints at Bluff Creek. Front pages of local papers, police reports, and plaster casts became this tradition's scripture; cryptozoology grew up around it, and Bigfoot, Ogopogo, and the Chupacabra traveled from rural hearsay to national news to town statues and annual festivals.

The internet accelerated the continent's myth-making to a matter of days. Two photoshopped images on the Something Awful forums on June 10, 2009 produced Slender Man; a single composite photograph by Trevor Henderson raised Siren Head in 2018; an anonymous paragraph on 4chan in May 2019 opened the yellow rooms of the Backrooms. These figures have exact creators and birthdates, yet they are rewritten by millions of hands like genuine folklore. From Bloody Mary's bathroom mirror to the black-eyed children asking to be let in, the temperament holds steady: these stories happen at your own front door, and the teller always insists a friend of a friend saw it happen.
